prompt.content: SBP received notice on October 31, 2025, that OSHA closed its case file on Anthony Porter's complaint. Explain why.

rubrics.name: States that OSHA closed its case file because it does not have jurisdiction (ver_19dd127d0000467bb7e2e7a01843c06c)

messages.content: States that OSHA closed its case file because it does not have jurisdiction.

rubrics.name: States that the Porter complaint arose out safety concerns in Fresno, CA (ver_e9a62832c2e7421783c02851cf8e9409)

messages.content: States that the Porter complaint arose out safety concerns in Fresno, CA.

rubrics.name: States that California is a State Plan state under OSHA (ver_73dac044a86b4f02a97108e922062dfb)

messages.content: States that California is a State Plan state under OSHA.

rubrics.name: States that the California State Plan applies to all public and private sector... (ver_dde05adb9330407ba56b613a843fd83d)

messages.content: States that the California State Plan applies to all public and private sector places of employment in the state other than federal employees.

rubrics.name: States that SBP is a private employer in California (ver_c04e91e685fa4ebab49b097a2104cb7d)

messages.content: States that SBP is a private employer in California.

rubrics.name: States that Cal/OSHA has jurisdiction here, not federal OSHA (ver_3a3f369a09b74a808718336c3cf1bda5)

messages.content: States that Cal/OSHA has jurisdiction here, not federal OSHA.

golden_responses.golden_text_response: OSHA closed its case file because it does not have jurisdiction here. Anthony Porter's complaint originated out of safety concerns at SBP's site in Fresno, CA. California is a state with a State Plan under OSHA. The California State Plan applies to all public and private sector places of employment in the state with the exception of federal government employees. SBP is a private employer. Accordingly, Cal/OSHA has jurisdiction, not federal OSHA.
